summary: Backend-only regression test for P0 monolith decomposition. Validated 5 newly-created modular routers (fipe.py, usdt.py, provider_schedule.py, orders.py, payment_methods.py) plus regression of non-migrated endpoints. 29/30 tests PASS (96.7%). Single failure is a PRE-EXISTING bug in /api/master/all-transactions (still in server.py, NOT migrated) — MongoDB ObjectId JSON serialization. All 5 migrated routers work correctly with shared deps.py auth layer. Auth tokens, role-based 403s, and FIPE/USDT/orders/payment-methods/provider endpoints all respond as designed. No duplicate-route shadowing detected on /api/usdt/rate (returns real numeric rate 5.85, not mock 5.45).

minor.issue: 500 Internal Server Error - ValueError: ObjectId is not iterable / jsonable_encoder fails. Backend log shows MongoDB _id leaking into response. Endpoint is still in server.py (line 3122, NOT migrated). Pre-existing bug, unrelated to this refactor.

action_items: (Optional / pre-existing) Fix /api/master/all-transactions ObjectId serialization in server.py line ~3122 — exclude _id or use jsonable_encoder with custom_encoder for ObjectId.
Consider migrating /api/master/all-transactions into routes/master.py during the next decomposition batch and fixing _id in the same change.
critical_code_review_comments: All 5 new routers correctly use shared deps.get_current_user / get_current_master_user — no duplicate auth logic.
/api/usdt/rate returns real rate (5.85) from USDTService, confirming no duplicate-route shadowing with the previous mock 5.45 value.
Role gating works: non-master gets 403 on /api/master/usdt/pending-approvals; client gets 403 on PATCH /api/user/payment-methods (lojista/service_provider only).

server.py is still 8734 lines / 98 endpoints — recommend continuing decomposition in batches (wallet/transactions, master CRUD, admin) and prioritising the all-transactions endpoint which has a serialization bug.
FIPE endpoints respond 200 even when external BrasilAPI is unavailable (gracefully returns success:false) — good resilience.
Provider availability returns 403 for clients (expected) instead of misleading 500 — auth wiring through deps.py is correct.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_routes_migration_p0.py (created — 30 pytest tests covering auth, 5 migrated routers, and 8 regression endpoints)
seed_data_creation: None — used existing master & franchise demo accounts.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Refactor regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_routes_migration_p0.py and loads REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env automatically. Run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_routes_migration_p0.py -v. Known persistent failure: TestRegression::test_master_all_transactions (pre-existing ObjectId serialization bug in server.py line ~3122) — keep this test as a watchdog until main agent fixes/migrates that endpoint. The 5 migrated routers (fipe, usdt, provider_schedule, orders, payment_methods) are confirmed working end-to-end.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: ValueError: [TypeError("'ObjectId' object is not iterable"), TypeError('vars() argument must have __dict__ attribute')] raised by fastapi.encoders.jsonable_encoder. The handler returns raw Mongo documents containing _id (bson.ObjectId) which FastAPI cannot serialize.
rca_of_the_issue.reproduction: 1) POST /api/auth/login as master → grab access_token. 2) GET /api/master/all-transactions with Bearer token → returns 500.
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: In server.py around line 3122, either (a) project away _id in the find() query (.find(query, {'_id': 0})), or (b) iterate results and `doc.pop('_id', None)` before returning, or (c) convert _id to str(doc['_id']). Also recommend migrating this endpoint into routes/master.py in the next batch.
